metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what constitutes a 'corrupt' store entry for `get_baseline` (e.g., 'a non-None return that is not a dict, or a dict missing required keys X/Y') so the detection branch and its test are unambiguously implementable.
findings.finding: The plan requires 'explicit corrupt-store detection' for the fail-closed path but never defines what constitutes a corrupt store entry, leaving the executor to invent the definition.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — 'Phase-3 consumer swap' bullet: 'add an explicit corrupt-store detection (get_baseline cannot itself signal "corrupt") so fail-closed is preserved'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical: 'corrupt defined as: get_baseline returns a non-None, non-dict value OR a dict failing schema validation (required keys: …); map to abort with fail-closed log event.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line (or extend the existing census AC) that verifies the DROP of `binding_walk_census` has occurred AND document the rollback path for the drop step (e.g., re-add the key or hold the drop behind a separate release gate).
findings.finding: The expand-contract plan for the census key explicitly ends with DROPPING `binding_walk_census`, but no AC line verifies that the drop has occurred. E1 requires migrations verify BOTH removal and replacement; T4 requires a destructive removal to have an explicit rollback/back-out path. The AC only gates 'emits both keys' and 'consumers migrated', leaving the removal step unverified and its rollback undescribed.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — census key expand-contract item; AC line 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to `decision_census`...'
findings.suggested_fix: Split the census AC into two lines: (1) 'Census emits BOTH `decision_census` and `binding_walk_census`; consumers migrated to `decision_census`' (verifiable mid-release) and (2) '`binding_walk_census` key removed; no registered consumer references it (verified by grep/test); rollback is re-adding the key in a patch release'. Add a note that the drop is a separate, independently reviewable release step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit field-name mapping step to the Phase-3 consumer swap spec: before calling `_local_matches_prev(field_name, ...)` with `get_baseline` output, map local names to Jira names (e.g. `'title' -> 'summary'`) so the baseline dict lookup resolves correctly; document this mapping requirement in the AC line for the consumer swap.
findings.finding: The plan directs re-pointing `_local_matches_prev` from `prev_snapshot` to `BindingStore.get_baseline`, but `_local_matches_prev` is called with `field_name` drawn from `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` which uses LOCAL names (e.g. `"title"`), while `get_baseline` returns a dict keyed by JIRA names (e.g. `"summary"`) — as stored by `_BASELINE_FIELDS` = `("summary", "description", ...)`. The plan mentions 'reconcile the two field-name sets' only in the context of the property test, not as an explicit implementation requirement for the consumer swap. A naïve re-point will silently fail to find `"title"` in the baseline dict (which contains only `"summary"`), degrading every title comparison to local-wins regardless of the flag.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Phase-3 consumer swap; AC line: '_local_matches_prev reads get_baseline behind it'
findings.suggested_fix: In the consumer-swap implementation, introduce a `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_FIELD = {'title': 'summary', ...}` mapping (or reuse the existing `_extract_jira_field` path) so that `_local_matches_prev` looks up `baseline.get(_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_FIELD.get(field_name, field_name), ...)` rather than `baseline.get(field_name, ...)` directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate BOTH `_local_matches_prev` call-sites in `outbound_fields.py` (lines 405 and 523) as swap targets, and specify the flag-read threading path (e.g. via `OutboundDiffConfig.prev_snapshot` replaced by a baseline-sourced value, or a new `use_baseline` bool on `_diff_fields`).
findings.finding: The plan says 're-point `outbound_fields._local_matches_prev` (and its `outbound_differ` caller)', but `_local_matches_prev` is called from within `outbound_fields._diff_fields` — not directly from `outbound_differ`. There are TWO call-sites in `outbound_fields.py`: line 405 (direction-suppression path) and line 523 (conflict-detection path in `_diff_fields`). The plan names neither call-site explicitly, and the flag-read threading path into `_diff_fields` (which has no config access today) is not specified.
findings.location: Remaining CODE / Phase-3 consumer swap; `outbound_fields.py:405` and `outbound_fields.py:523`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the field-name translation rule for the consumer swap: document whether `_local_matches_prev`/`get_baseline` must translate `field_name` from Jira-space (`summary`) to local-space (`title`) before the `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` guard, or whether `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` must be updated to Jira-space names, and make the property test exercise the same name-space the live `_diff_fields` loop uses.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the implementer to 'reconcile the two field-name sets `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` and `baseline_shadow._MIRRORED_FIELDS`' when writing the swapped consumer and its property test, but never specifies the reconciliation protocol: `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` uses LOCAL names (`title`, ...) while `_MIRRORED_FIELDS` uses JIRA names (`summary`, ...). The swap must look up the baseline by one name-space while `_local_matches_prev` receives the other — this translation rule is the entire correctness hinge of the swap and is not defined.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Phase-3 consumer swap; AC line: '_local_matches_prev reads get_baseline behind it; None->local-wins'
findings.suggested_fix: In the CODE section, add: 'Note: `_diff_fields` iterates `local_mapped` keys which are in Jira-space ("summary", not "title"); the `_INBOUND_MIRRORED_FIELDS` guard must therefore be updated to Jira-space names when the swap lands, OR a name-translation layer must map field_name before the baseline lookup. The property test must exercise the same field-name space as the live loop.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the corrupt-store detection protocol for the consumer swap: define whether the implementer should call a new `BindingStore` method (e.g., `has_corrupt_baseline(local_id) -> bool`), inspect the raw binding entry directly before calling `get_baseline`, or use a sentinel return value — and add this protocol to the AC.
findings.finding: The `get_baseline` method returns `None` for BOTH a legitimately absent baseline (no ancestor yet — local-wins is correct) AND a non-dict/corrupt baseline entry (fail-closed abort is required). The plan instructs 'map None -> the existing absent-baseline (local-wins) branch' AND 'add an explicit corrupt-store detection (get_baseline cannot itself signal corrupt)' but never specifies the detection protocol — i.e., what additional call, field read, or wrapper distinguishes these two cases so the fail-closed path can be reached.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Phase-3 consumer swap; AC: 'None->local-wins; corrupt-store fail-closed preserved (explicit detection)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the comparison protocol for `classifier_legacy_divergence`: enumerate which `DecisionKind` values are comparable to which legacy differ output types, specify which code paths permit per-binding extraction, and name the paths that must be documented as out-of-scope.
findings.finding: The `classifier_legacy_divergence` alerting CODE item specifies a processing protocol ('compare `classify()`'s per-binding decision against the PRODUCTION differ's action for that binding in the SAME pass') but never defines which code paths permit extraction of a per-binding production decision, nor the comparison mapping between `DecisionKind` enum values and 'differ actions'. The plan says 'scope the alert to the paths where it is' without enumerating those paths — the decision rule is not co-located with the alert schema.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting; AC: 'classifier_legacy_divergence emitted with the typed fields above (or scoped+documented where per-binding legacy decisions aren't extractable)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit proving commands (unit tests or pytest parametrize cases) for (a) `get_baseline` returning `None` → local-wins branch selected, and (b) `get_baseline` returning a corrupt value → abort is triggered and logged, and reference them in the AC.
findings.finding: No proving command exists to verify the None->local-wins branch or the corrupt-store fail-closed abort path after the Phase-3 consumer swap implementation; the property test covers direction-preservation but not these two distinct code branches.
findings.location: AC: '[CODE] `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap` flag added + documented… None->local-wins; corrupt-store fail-closed preserved (explicit detection)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'Unit tests `test_consumer_swap_none_baseline_local_wins` and `test_consumer_swap_corrupt_baseline_abort` pass (pytest -k "none_baseline or corrupt_baseline" exits 0 and log captures fail-closed event).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the rollback procedure to explicitly state whether the pre-heal backup (Jira JSON + git tag) must be restored after flag-revert, and under what condition (e.g. if heal mutations occurred, restore both sides before the dry-run confirmation).
findings.finding: The rollback procedure (flag-revert + dry-run) does not specify whether binding-store mutations written during the live heal need to be restored from the pre-heal backup after rollback, leaving a potential silent data inconsistency between the store and Jira.
findings.location: [OPERATIONAL] Flip the flag only after BOTH gates / AC: 'rollback rehearsed (flag-revert + dry-run run-ids recorded)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a rollback decision tree: if the live heal executed any mutations before rollback was triggered, restore the binding-store from the pre-heal git tag and re-apply the Jira JSON export; then perform the flag-revert and dry-run confirmation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [E5] Add explicit unit tests for the two new sentinel paths in `_local_matches_prev`: (a) `get_baseline` returns `None` → assert local-wins branch is taken, and (b) corrupt-store condition detected → assert fail-closed abort is raised, to ensure the safety guarantee is verified before the flag flip.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new conditional branches in `_local_matches_prev` — specifically (a) `None` → local-wins and (b) corrupt-store → fail-closed abort — but specifies no tests for these failure/edge paths; only the happy-path direction-preservation property test is mandated.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — consumer swap bullet; AC line: 'None->local-wins; corrupt-store fail-closed preserved (explicit detection)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add two focused unit tests (not property tests) alongside the existing direction-preservation test: one that injects a None return from `get_baseline` and asserts the local-wins branch fires, and one that injects a corrupt-store sentinel and asserts the abort/fail-closed path fires.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file and function where `SyncLogger.log('decision_census', **census(...))` should be added (e.g. `run_differs._run_differs_binding_walk` alongside the existing `binding_walk_census` emit) so the expand-contract task has an unambiguous edit target.
findings.finding: The plan's census expand-contract task says 'emit BOTH `decision_census` and `binding_walk_census`' but no `SyncLogger.log('decision_census', ...)` call currently exists anywhere in the codebase — only `binding_walk_census` is emitted (`run_differs.py:652`). `classify.py:351` documents the intended emit but the actual call is absent. The plan does not name the file/function where the new `decision_census` log call should be added.
findings.location: Remaining CODE / Census key expand-contract; `run_differs.py:652` and `classify.py:351`
findings.suggested_fix: Add the `decision_census` emit in `run_differs._run_differs_binding_walk` immediately after or alongside `sync_logger.log('binding_walk_census', **walk.census)` at line 652, using the same `walk.census` dict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring at least one observed live reconciler pass with `baseline_consumer_swap=true` (workflow run-id recorded, `baseline_shadow_check divergent==0`, no unexpected mutations in reconciler log) before closing the ticket.
findings.finding: After the flag is flipped to true in production, no AC requires observing even one live reconciler pass running with `baseline_consumer_swap=true` and confirming zero divergence or zero unexpected mutations — the post-flip verification gap means the cutover path is never exercised end-to-end as a criterion.
findings.location: AC: '[OPERATIONAL] Flip only after both gates; rollback rehearsed (flag-revert + dry-run run-ids recorded)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '[OPERATIONAL] Post-flip: at least one live reconciler pass with `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap=true` observed clean (baseline_shadow_check divergent==0, zero unintended mutations); workflow run-id recorded.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify per-instance idempotency guards for the live heal: before mutating each drift instance, the agent must check its current state (e.g., Jira issue already Done / binding already retired) and skip if already healed, so a re-run after partial failure is safe.
findings.finding: The capped live heal is not specified as idempotent/resumable. If the agent-run heal aborts mid-flight (e.g., after healing 3 of 7 drift instances), re-running it has no documented safe-re-entry guarantee. The RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE (f8b5→REB-530) and ADOPT (REB-532) heal steps in particular may produce double-mutations if replayed naively — the plan does not instruct the agent to check already-healed state before mutating each instance.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict incl. the GC-after-grace deletion (f8b5-2f30 -> REB-530)'; AC '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the OPERATIONAL section: 'For each drift instance, the agent checks the current Jira state (via ACLI GET) and local binding state BEFORE applying the heal mutation; if the instance is already in the target state (Done / retired / adopted), skip it and log `heal_skipped` with reason=already_healed. This makes the full heal procedure idempotent and resumable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the artifact retention period explicitly in the plan (e.g., '90 days, configured via `actions/upload-artifact` `retention-days: 90`') rather than deferring to 'retention documented'.
findings.finding: Workflow artifact retention is deferred with 'retention documented' but no retention period or documentation location is given, making this a placeholder that blocks the OPERATIONAL AC from being fully verifiable.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — pre-heal backup bullet: 'Store the artifacts in the workflow run artifacts (retention documented)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'Store in workflow run artifacts with `retention-days: 90` (or the org default if >90); document in `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete artifact retention period (e.g., 90 days) and where the policy is documented (e.g., a `retention-days:` field in the workflow YAML), and add it to the AC as an observable proof.
findings.finding: Artifact retention for pre-heal backups is promised ('retention documented') but neither the retention period nor the location of that documentation is specified in the plan, leaving the retirement/cleanup path undefined.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal' section; AC: 'artifacts stored'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `retention-days: 90` to the workflow's artifact upload step and reference this in the AC as '`retention-days: 90` set in workflow YAML'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the explicit cap value (e.g., max N bindings mutated per heal run, or a percentage of total bindings) in the OPERATIONAL bullet and AC so the executor can assert `healed_count <= N` post-run.
findings.finding: The 'capped live heal' cap value is never quantified. The plan uses 'capped' as a modifier throughout but never states the cap (e.g., max N tickets mutated, max M API calls, or a percentage threshold). An executor cannot determine when the cap is reached or verify that it was respected.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict' bullet; AC line '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: Add e.g. 'cap: max 50 bindings per heal run (abort if heal_count would exceed cap before first mutation)' to the OPERATIONAL bullet and add `assert heal_count <= 50` (or equivalent log-query) to the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a structured proving command for `classifier_legacy_divergence` that asserts each typed field (e.g., `jq -e '.classifier_count | type == "number" and .divergent_pairs | type == "array"' <captured-log-event>` exits 0).
findings.finding: The `classifier_legacy_divergence` alert AC has no structured proving command that asserts the typed payload fields are present and correctly typed in the emitted log/event; 'emitted with the typed fields above' is not a verifiable check.
findings.location: AC: '[CODE] Census emits both keys… `classifier_legacy_divergence` emitted with the typed fields above (or scoped+documented…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'Verified by `jq -e \'(.classifier_count | type) == "number" and (.legacy_count | type) == "number" and (.divergent_pairs | type) == "array" and (.pass_id | type) == "string"\' <log-artifact>` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the RED evidence mechanism: e.g., 'Commit the test (without implementation) on a separate branch, run `pytest <test_name>` and record the failing CI run-id; then implement and record the passing run-id; both run-ids cited in the PR.'
findings.finding: The RED-first AC does not specify how the RED state is captured as evidence before implementation begins; without a stated mechanism (e.g., a CI run on a branch with the test but without the implementation), an executor may write the test after the implementation and retroactively claim RED, defeating the RED-first intent.
findings.location: AC: '[CODE] RED-first direction-preservation property test (swapped consumer, five mapped fields) RED before, GREEN after'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'RED evidence: failing pytest run-id on a pre-implementation branch; GREEN evidence: passing pytest run-id post-implementation. Both run-ids recorded in the PR description.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `get_baseline(local_id: str) -> dict | None` to `BindingStoreProtocol` in `outbound_differ.py` as a required edit target of the consumer swap.
findings.finding: `BindingStoreProtocol` in `outbound_differ.py` (line 216–220) declares only `get_jira_key` and `is_bound`; it does NOT include `get_baseline`. The consumer-swap re-points `_local_matches_prev` to use `get_baseline`, but `compute_outbound_mutations` accepts a `BindingStoreProtocol` — so either the protocol must be extended or the baseline must be pre-fetched and threaded separately. This required protocol update is not enumerated in the plan.
findings.location: Remaining CODE / Phase-3 consumer swap; `outbound_differ.py:216-220` (`BindingStoreProtocol`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `def get_baseline(self, local_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None: ...` to the `BindingStoreProtocol` class in `outbound_differ.py`, and update any test doubles/mocks that satisfy the protocol.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the post-rollback binding-store schema state: assert explicitly that `baseline` entries in `bindings.json` accumulated by Phase-1 shadow are valid and inert when `baseline_consumer_swap=false`, require no cleanup, and are safe to leave in place for re-enablement.
findings.finding: The plan does not assert the binding-store schema state after flag rollback. After reverting `reconciler.baseline_consumer_swap` to `false`, the `baseline` fields accumulated in `bindings.json` by Phase-1 shadow persist in the store. The plan's rollback rehearsal verifies the reconciler 'reverts to `prev_snapshot` arbitration with no divergence' but does not state whether those orphaned `baseline` entries in the store are a valid intermediate state or require explicit cleanup.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Flip only after BOTH gates. Rollback rehearsed: (a) revert the flag to false and confirm the reconciler reverts to `prev_snapshot` arbitration with no divergence'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the rollback rehearsal: '(c) confirm that `baseline` entries in `bindings.json` are inert when the flag is false (get_baseline is never called), require no removal, and the store schema is valid for re-enabling the flag in a future pass — cite the ADR 0026 §Consequences note as evidence.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific Jira API surface for the pre-heal export (e.g., `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}` via `AcliClient.get_issue_by_rest` for each of the 7 affected keys) and specify the abort condition (e.g., any non-2xx response or missing `fields` key aborts the heal), so the backup step's failure mode is verifiable.
findings.finding: The OPERATIONAL pre-heal bilateral backup step asserts 'export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API)' but does not name the specific API surface (endpoint path or ACLI subcommand) the agent will use. The codebase offers two candidate paths — `AcliClient.get_issue_by_rest` (GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}, immediately consistent) and `acli_cli_ops.get_issue` (JQL search, subject to index lag) — with materially different consistency and error-surface properties. Because the AC says 'heal aborts if either backup fails', the abort condition depends on which invocation is used; an unspecified surface cannot be verified for failure-mode coverage.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL section: 'PRE-HEAL BACKUP covers BOTH sides: export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API)' / AC: '[OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup (Jira JSON + tickets-branch tag); heal aborts if either backup fails'
findings.suggested_fix: In the OPERATIONAL section and matching AC, replace 'export affected Jira issue state (JSON via Jira API)' with the concrete call: 'for each of the 7 affected keys, call `AcliClient.get_issue_by_rest(key)` (GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}) and assert `fields` is present; any HTTPError or missing `fields` aborts the heal'. This pins the immediately-consistent endpoint, makes the abort condition unambiguous, and matches the already-verified REST mixin.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit instruction to pin the reconcile-bridge workflow to `--mode dry-run` (via the `workflow_dispatch` mode input or by disabling the scheduled trigger) for the duration of the live heal, and restore it afterward — mirroring the pause already mandated for A3's live remediation pass.
findings.finding: The 20-minute reconciler is not paused during the live heal window. The coordination note only pauses the reconciler for A3's live remediation pass, not for B1's capped live heal. Concurrent reconciler passes running during the heal can observe partially-healed binding-store state and emit conflicting mutations (e.g., re-classifying a partially-retired RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE binding, or re-issuing an ADOPT for REB-532 while the agent heal is in flight), undermining the bilateral backup's coverage.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'Capped live heal under bootstrap-strict'; 'COORDINATION: A3's live remediation pauses the reconciler and will reset this streak — do NOT run A3's live pass during the streak window'
findings.suggested_fix: In the OPERATIONAL 'Capped live heal' bullet, add: 'Before beginning any heal mutation, pin the reconcile-bridge workflow to dry-run (set the `mode` input to `dry-run` or disable the schedule); restore to `live` only after the heal completes and the next-pass census confirms 0 acting decisions.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'one release' with a concrete, measurable milestone (e.g., 'one full deploy cycle with >=N successful reconciler passes emitting both keys, confirmed by log query') so the migration step has a deterministic trigger.
findings.finding: 'For one release' in the census expand-contract step is undefined — 'release' is not defined in the plan (a deploy? a sprint? a version tag?), so an executor cannot determine when it is safe to proceed to the migration step.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — 'Census key: expand-contract' bullet: 'emit BOTH… for one release'
findings.suggested_fix: State: 'emit BOTH keys for at least 48 h of production traffic (verified by log query showing both keys present in the last 144 reconciler passes), then migrate consumers.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a consumer inventory step to the census expand-contract: before dropping `binding_walk_census`, enumerate all consumers (GHA workflow log-greps, external dashboards, monitoring rules) and confirm each has migrated to `decision_census`.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract CODE item instructs 'migrate consumers to `decision_census`, then drop `binding_walk_census`' but never enumerates what consumers of `binding_walk_census` exist beyond the single in-repo emit at `run_differs.py:652`. Without a consumer inventory, an executor cannot determine when migration is complete or whether external consumers (GHA log parsers, ops dashboards, monitoring rules) depend on the old key.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Census key expand-contract; AC: 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to `decision_census`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the `binding_walk_census` → `decision_census` migration in a changelog entry or `docs/` note: define 'one release' as a concrete milestone, list known consumers to migrate, and add a tracking issue for the drop step.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract migration (emit both `decision_census` and `binding_walk_census`, migrate consumers, then drop `binding_walk_census`) has no documented timeline, tracking issue, or migration guide. If the drop step is indefinitely deferred, consumers that haven't migrated will silently lose data when it is eventually removed, and the temporary dual-emit state becomes permanent dead weight.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Census key: expand-contract; AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph migration note to `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` or a CHANGELOG entry: 'binding_walk_census is deprecated as of <release>; will be removed after <next release>. Consumers: <list>.' Reference a tracking ticket for the drop.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add retry/timeout spec for the Jira API export step in the pre-heal backup and instrument backup-step failures with an explicit log/alert so operators can distinguish transient vs. permanent backup failures.
findings.finding: The pre-heal Jira API export (bilateral backup) has no retry, timeout, or observability specification for the case where the Jira API is slow or temporarily unavailable during backup.
findings.location: [OPERATIONAL] Capped live heal / AC: 'Capped live heal with a bilateral pre-heal backup'
findings.suggested_fix: Specify: up to N retries with exponential backoff (e.g. 3 retries, 5s/10s/20s) for the Jira export call; a hard timeout (e.g. 60s total); emit a `pre_heal_backup_failure {reason, step, pass_id}` alert on abort so the cause is observable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an enforcement mechanism for the A3/streak mutual exclusion — e.g., a GHA concurrency group shared between the A3 workflow and `reconcile-bridge.yml`, or a documented check (query A3 run timestamps against the streak window) that must be verified before the flip is executed.
findings.finding: The A3 coordination requirement ('do NOT run A3's live pass during the streak window') is advisory only — no enforcement mechanism is specified, so an inadvertent A3 run would silently corrupt the streak and allow the flag flip to proceed on a false gate.
findings.location: Remaining OPERATIONAL — 'COORDINATION' note; AC line for >=10 consecutive clean shadow passes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the ACLI_SHA256 posture for the live-heal execution path: either require `ACLI_SHA256` to be set as a prerequisite for the heal (fail the workflow step if unset, not just warn), or explicitly document in the plan that the deploy team has accepted the warn-only posture with rationale.
findings.finding: The plan's new OPERATIONAL steps (capped live heal, >=10 consecutive clean shadow passes) run through the GHA workflow, which downloads and executes the acli binary from acli.atlassian.com. The workflow's ACLI_SHA256 checksum verification is declared as '⚠️ strongly recommended' but optional — the workflow warns and proceeds without it. The plan does not declare or require that ACLI_SHA256 is set before the live-heal execution, leaving the supply-chain posture of the boundary-crossing path undeclared in the plan's security contract.
findings.location: OPERATIONAL section (capped live heal, streak gate); docs/jira-sync-setup.md reference table (ACLI_SHA256 row); .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml lines 156-160
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's OPERATIONAL prerequisites for the capped live heal, add: 'Confirm `ACLI_SHA256` is set in repo Variables before running the heal pass — the workflow step that downloads acli will fail if unset (upgrade the warning to an error for heal-mode runs, or gate the heal dispatch on its presence).' In the workflow, consider promoting the ACLI_SHA256 check from a warning to an error (exit 1) to make the posture unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split the bundled census+classifier AC line into two independent AC lines: one for the census expand-contract (emit both keys, migrate consumers, drop old key) and one for the classifier-legacy divergence alert (typed fields, scoped paths documented).
findings.finding: The single AC line 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to `decision_census`; `classifier_legacy_divergence` emitted...' bundles two independent CODE deliverables (census expand-contract and classifier divergence alerting) into one criterion. This violates E1's requirement that each deliverable be independently coverable, and makes the criterion ambiguous: partial completion of one deliverable could block sign-off of the other.
findings.location: AC line: 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract), consumers migrated to `decision_census`; `classifier_legacy_divergence` emitted with the typed fields above...'
findings.suggested_fix: AC line A: '[CODE] Census expand-contract complete: both `decision_census` and `binding_walk_census` emitted; all consumers migrated to `decision_census`; `binding_walk_census` dropped and verified absent.' AC line B: '[CODE] `classifier_legacy_divergence` alert emitted with typed fields {classifier_count, legacy_count, divergent_pairs, pass_id}; any code paths where per-binding legacy decisions are not extractable are scoped and documented.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or `docs/` design-doc section (separate from the operational flip-procedure) capturing: *why* BindingStore replaces prev_snapshot, the None→local-wins and corrupt→fail-closed contracts, and the rollback safety invariant.
findings.finding: The Phase-3 consumer swap — re-pointing `_local_matches_prev` from `prev_snapshot` to `BindingStore.get_baseline` — is a novel architectural decision about arbitration data source, including the None→local-wins and corrupt→fail-closed contracts. The plan routes this only to an operational flag-flip doc (`docs/jira-sync-setup.md`), not an ADR or design-doc section capturing the rationale, failure-mode analysis, and rollback contract.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — Phase-3 consumer swap; AC line 1; `docs/jira-sync-setup.md` reference
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/NNN-baseline-consumer-swap.md` (or a dedicated section in an existing architecture doc) covering: motivation, the two failure-mode mappings, the rollback contract, and a pointer to the flag in `_config_schema.py`. Reference it from `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a fail-safe for the streak log query: if fewer than N events are found in the expected window (or the query errors), treat the streak as reset-to-zero and emit a `streak_query_incomplete` alert rather than computing a partial streak.
findings.finding: The log-derived streak computation ('query the last N baseline_shadow_check events') has no specified fallback or instrumentation if the log store is unavailable or returns incomplete data, creating a risk of a silently incorrect streak count.
findings.location: [OPERATIONAL] >=10 CONSECUTIVE clean shadow passes / AC: 'streak is DERIVED from the sync-log history'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a check: if the log query returns fewer events than the look-back window implies (accounting for the 20-min cadence), emit `streak_query_incomplete {events_found, window_minutes, pass_id}` and reset the streak to 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency/atomicity contract for BindingStore reads (`get_baseline`) vs. concurrent writes — e.g., confirm reads are transactional/atomic, or add a GHA concurrency group (`concurrency: group: reconcile, cancel-in-progress: false`) to serialize runs, and document which mechanism is relied upon.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify atomicity or concurrency safety for BindingStore reads (`get_baseline`) vs. concurrent writes when two GHA workflow runs overlap (the reconciler runs every 20 min and a run could exceed 20 min).
findings.location: Remaining CODE — 'Phase-3 consumer swap' section; also `reconcile-bridge.yml` every-20-min note
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `concurrency: group: reconcile-live\n  cancel-in-progress: false` block to `reconcile-bridge.yml` to serialize runs, and note this in the plan so the atomicity assumption is explicit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either expand the plan title to name all four code deliverables (or group them as 'supporting observability prerequisites'), or explicitly mark census and classifier-alerting as non-gating with a rationale, so their relationship to the flip gate is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan body includes two work items — census expand-contract migration and classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting — that are not named in the title's three stated deliverables, representing undeclared scope additions.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — 'Census key' and 'classifier-vs-legacy divergence alerting' sections; plan title

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] [G5] Consider whether the census expand-contract migration (emit both keys → migrate consumers → drop old key) should be extracted into a separate child ticket, given it is independently releasable and has its own API-consumer-facing reason to change, separate from the Phase-3 consumer swap concern.
findings.finding: The census expand-contract migration (emit both `binding_walk_census` and `decision_census`, migrate consumers, then drop the old key) is independently releasable and has its own distinct reason to change — it affects API consumers of the census key regardless of the consumer-swap outcome — yet it is bundled with the Phase-3 consumer swap and divergence alerting.
findings.location: Remaining CODE — census key expand-contract bullet; AC line: 'Census emits both keys (expand-contract)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
